author: George Orwell
synopsis: In a totalitarian future, Winston Smith struggles against a regime that controls truth, history, and even thought itself.
quote: War is peace. Freedom is slavery. Ignorance is strength.

title: Animal Farm
author: George Orwell
synopsis: Farm animals overthrow their human owner only to fall under the rule of pigs who grow just as tyrannical.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
synopsis: A genetically engineered society pursues happiness through pleasure and conformity, at the cost of individuality.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
synopsis: A reluctant hobbit, Bilbo Baggins, sets out across Middle-earth with a band of dwarves to reclaim their mountain home from a dragon.
quote: Not all those who wander are lost.

title: The Fellowship of the Ring
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
synopsis: A hobbit and his companions set out from the Shire to destroy a powerful ring before it falls into the hands of a dark lord.

title: The Two Towers
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
synopsis: The fellowship splits apart as war spreads across Middle-earth and two hobbits press on toward Mordor.

title: The Return of the King
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
synopsis: The final battle for Middle-earth unfolds as the ring-bearer nears Mount Doom and old kingdoms rise again.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
synopsis: An orphaned boy discovers he is a wizard and begins his first year at a magical school full of secrets.

title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
author: J.K. Rowling
synopsis: A mysterious force begins attacking students at Hogwarts, and Harry must uncover the truth behind a long-hidden chamber.

title: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
author: J.K. Rowling
synopsis: An escaped prisoner is rumored to be hunting Harry, who uncovers long-buried secrets about his past.

title: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
author: C.S. Lewis
synopsis: Four siblings step through a wardrobe into a snowbound land ruled by a witch and awaiting the return of a lion king.

title: A Game of Thrones
author: George R.R. Martin
synopsis: Noble houses vie for the Iron Throne while an ancient enemy stirs beyond a great wall in the north.

title: The Name of the Wind
author: Patrick Rothfuss
synopsis: A legendary figure recounts his rise from a gifted but troubled youth to one of the most feared men in the world.

title: American Gods
author: Neil Gaiman
synopsis: A man caught between old gods and new finds himself at the center of a brewing storm across modern America.

title: Good Omens
author: Terry Pratchett & Neil Gaiman
synopsis: An angel and a demon team up to stop the apocalypse after a clerical mix-up misplaces the Antichrist.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
synopsis: A disillusioned teenager wanders New York City over a few days, grappling with alienation and loss.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
synopsis: A young girl in the segregated South watches her father defend a Black man falsely accused of a crime.

title: Of Mice and Men
author: John Steinbeck
synopsis: Two displaced ranch workers dream of owning their own land during the hardships of the Great Depression.

title: The Grapes of Wrath
author: John Steinbeck
synopsis: A family of tenant farmers travels west during the Dust Bowl in search of work and dignity.

title: Gone with the Wind
author: Margaret Mitchell
synopsis: A headstrong Southern woman navigates love and survival against the backdrop of the Civil War and its aftermath.

title: The Old Man and the Sea
author: Ernest Hemingway
synopsis: An aging fisherman battles a giant marlin far out at sea in a test of endurance and pride.

title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
author: Gabriel Garcia Marquez
synopsis: Generations of the Buendia family live out triumph and tragedy in the isolated town of Macondo.

title: Lord of the Flies
author: William Golding
synopsis: Stranded on a deserted island, a group of boys descends into savagery as their attempts at order collapse.

title: Catch-22
author: Joseph Heller
synopsis: A bombardier in World War II tries every absurd trick imaginable to be declared unfit for combat.

title: The Bell Jar
author: Sylvia Plath
synopsis: A young woman's promising future unravels as she struggles with her mental health in 1950s America.

title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
author: Ken Kesey
synopsis: A rebellious new patient upends the rigid routine of a psychiatric ward run by a controlling nurse.

title: Beloved
author: Toni Morrison
synopsis: A formerly enslaved woman is haunted by the past in the form of a mysterious young woman who arrives at her home.

title: The Color Purple
author: Alice Walker
synopsis: Told through letters, a woman's life in the early twentieth-century South reveals resilience amid hardship.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
synopsis: A shepherd boy travels from Spain to Egypt in search of a treasure foretold by a recurring dream.

title: Life of Pi
author: Yann Martel
synopsis: A boy survives a shipwreck and spends months adrift on a lifeboat with a Bengal tiger.

title: The Kite Runner
author: Khaled Hosseini
synopsis: A boy's betrayal of his closest friend in Kabul haunts him for decades as Afghanistan is transformed by war.

title: A Thousand Splendid Suns
author: Khaled Hosseini
synopsis: Two women from different generations forge an unlikely bond as their lives are shaped by decades of conflict.

title: The Book Thief
author: Markus Zusak
synopsis: A girl growing up in Nazi Germany finds comfort in stolen books while sheltering a secret in her basement.

title: All the Light We Cannot See
author: Anthony Doerr
synopsis: A blind French girl and a German soldier cross paths during the Allied invasion of occupied France.

title: The Goldfinch
author: Donna Tartt
synopsis: After a museum bombing kills his mother, a boy clings to a stolen painting as he drifts through a turbulent life.

title: Where the Crawdads Sing
author: Delia Owens
synopsis: A girl raised alone in the marshes of North Carolina becomes a suspect when a local man is found dead.

title: Fight Club
author: Chuck Palahniuk
synopsis: An insomniac office worker forms an underground fight club that spirals into something far more dangerous.

title: The Fault in Our Stars
author: John Green
synopsis: Two teenagers with cancer fall in love while grappling with mortality, meaning, and the books they share.

title: No Country for Old Men
author: Cormac McCarthy
synopsis: A cash windfall from a drug deal gone wrong sets a relentless killer on the trail of an ordinary man.

title: American Psycho
author: Bret Easton Ellis
synopsis: A wealthy New York investment banker hides a violent double life behind a veneer of status and routine.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
synopsis: On the desert planet Arrakis, Paul Atreides rises amid prophecy, betrayal, and the politics of a galaxy-spanning empire.

title: Foundation
author: Isaac Asimov
synopsis: A mathematician devises a plan to preserve civilization through the coming collapse of a galactic empire.

title: I, Robot
author: Isaac Asimov
synopsis: A series of interconnected stories explores the rise of robots and the laws meant to keep them in check.

title: Slaughterhouse-Five
author: Kurt Vonnegut
synopsis: A soldier becomes unstuck in time, reliving moments of his life out of order, including the bombing of Dresden.

title: Ender's Game
author: Orson Scott Card
synopsis: A gifted child is trained at a military academy to lead humanity's defense against an alien threat.

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
author: Douglas Adams
synopsis: Moments before Earth's destruction, an ordinary man is whisked off on a chaotic tour of the galaxy.

title: Neuromancer
author: William Gibson
synopsis: A washed-up hacker is hired for one last job that plunges him into a dangerous virtual underworld.

title: Snow Crash
author: Neal Stephenson
synopsis: A pizza-delivery hacker investigates a digital drug that threatens both the virtual and real worlds.

title: Jurassic Park
author: Michael Crichton
synopsis: A theme park populated with cloned dinosaurs descends into chaos when its safety systems fail.

title: The Andromeda Strain
author: Michael Crichton
synopsis: Scientists race to contain a deadly extraterrestrial microorganism brought back by a crashed satellite.

title: The Martian
author: Andy Weir
synopsis: An astronaut stranded alone on Mars must use ingenuity and grit to survive until rescue is possible.

title: Ready Player One
author: Ernest Cline
synopsis: In a virtual reality-obsessed future, a teenager hunts for a hidden prize left behind by the creator of a global game.

title: Fahrenheit 451
author: Ray Bradbury
synopsis: In a future where books are outlawed, a fireman whose job is to burn them begins to question everything.

title: A Clockwork Orange
author: Anthony Burgess
synopsis: A violent young delinquent undergoes a controversial treatment meant to strip him of free will.

title: The Handmaid's Tale
author: Margaret Atwood
synopsis: In a theocratic regime, a woman is stripped of her rights and forced into reproductive servitude.

title: The Hunger Games
author: Suzanne Collins
synopsis: A teenage girl volunteers for a televised fight to the death to spare her younger sister.

title: Divergent
author: Veronica Roth
synopsis: In a society divided into rigid factions, a girl discovers she doesn't fit neatly into any of them.

title: The Maze Runner
author: James Dashner
synopsis: A boy wakes with no memory inside a vast maze, where escape means confronting deadly creatures and hidden secrets.

title: The Road
author: Cormac McCarthy
synopsis: A father and son journey across a devastated landscape, holding onto hope in a world stripped of almost everything.

title: The Shining
author: Stephen King
synopsis: A family caretaking an isolated hotel for the winter is slowly consumed by the malevolent forces within it.

title: It
author: Stephen King
synopsis: A group of childhood friends confronts an ancient shape-shifting evil that returns to their town decades later.

title: Carrie
author: Stephen King
synopsis: A bullied teenager with telekinetic powers is pushed past her breaking point at her high school prom.

title: The Stand
author: Stephen King
synopsis: Survivors of a global pandemic split into opposing camps as good and evil prepare for a final confrontation.

title: Misery
author: Stephen King
synopsis: An injured novelist is held captive by an obsessive fan who forces him to write for her alone.

title: Interview with the Vampire
author: Anne Rice
synopsis: A centuries-old vampire recounts his transformation and the moral torment that has followed him ever since.

title: The Exorcist
author: William Peter Blatty
synopsis: A mother seeks help from priests when her young daughter appears to be possessed by a malevolent spirit.

title: Rosemary's Baby
author: Ira Levin
synopsis: A young woman grows increasingly suspicious of her neighbors and her own pregnancy after moving into a new apartment.

title: World War Z
author: Max Brooks
synopsis: Survivors from around the world recount their experiences of a global war against the undead.

title: Twilight
author: Stephenie Meyer
synopsis: A teenage girl falls for a mysterious classmate who turns out to be a vampire with a dangerous past.

title: The Notebook
author: Nicholas Sparks
synopsis: A man reads the story of two young lovers separated by class and circumstance to a woman in a nursing home.

title: Outlander
author: Diana Gabaldon
synopsis: A woman from the 1940s is mysteriously transported back to eighteenth-century Scotland, where she falls in love.

title: Me Before You
author: Jojo Moyes
synopsis: A young woman becomes caregiver to a former adventurer left paralyzed by an accident, and the two form an unexpected bond.

title: The Da Vinci Code
author: Dan Brown
synopsis: A symbologist races to decode a trail of clues hidden in art and history after a murder at the Louvre.

title: Angels & Demons
author: Dan Brown
synopsis: A symbologist is called to the Vatican to stop a secret society's plot involving a stolen weapon of mass destruction.

title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
author: Stieg Larsson
synopsis: A journalist and a brilliant hacker team up to investigate a decades-old disappearance with dark family secrets.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
synopsis: When a woman vanishes on her anniversary, suspicion falls on her husband as the truth proves far stranger than expected.

title: The Silence of the Lambs
author: Thomas Harris
synopsis: A young FBI trainee seeks the help of an imprisoned killer to catch another serial murderer on the loose.

title: And Then There Were None
author: Agatha Christie
synopsis: Ten strangers are lured to an isolated island where they are killed off one by one according to a sinister rhyme.

title: Murder on the Orient Express
author: Agatha Christie
synopsis: A detective must identify a killer among the passengers of a luxury train stranded by snow.

title: The Girl on the Train
author: Paula Hawkins
synopsis: A woman's daily train commute draws her into the investigation of a neighbor's disappearance.

title: The Diary of a Young Girl
author: Anne Frank
synopsis: A teenager's diary documents her family's two years in hiding during the Nazi occupation of the Netherlands.

title: Night
author: Elie Wiesel
synopsis: A survivor recounts his experience as a teenager in Nazi concentration camps during the Holocaust.

title: Long Walk to Freedom
author: Nelson Mandela
synopsis: Nelson Mandela recounts his life from rural childhood through decades of imprisonment to the presidency of South Africa.

title: Into the Wild
author: Jon Krakauer
synopsis: A young man abandons his possessions and comfortable life to live off the land in the Alaskan wilderness, with tragic results.

title: Unbroken
author: Laura Hillenbrand
synopsis: An Olympic runner survives a plane crash, weeks adrift at sea, and brutal captivity as a prisoner of war.

title: Steve Jobs
author: Walter Isaacson
synopsis: A comprehensive biography of the Apple co-founder, drawn from interviews with Jobs and those around him.

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
synopsis: Raised off the grid with no formal schooling, the author describes her journey to a university education and beyond.

title: Eat, Pray, Love
author: Elizabeth Gilbert
synopsis: After a difficult divorce, a woman travels through Italy, India, and Indonesia in search of balance and meaning.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
synopsis: A sweeping look at how Homo sapiens came to dominate the planet, from the cognitive revolution to the present day.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
synopsis: A landmark explanation of cosmology, black holes, and the nature of time written for a general audience.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
synopsis: An influential argument that genes, not individuals or species, are the primary drivers of evolution.

title: Into Thin Air
author: Jon Krakauer
synopsis: A firsthand account of a disastrous 1996 expedition to climb Mount Everest that claimed several lives.

title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
author: Rebecca Skloot
synopsis: The story of the woman whose cells, taken without her knowledge, became one of the most important tools in medicine.

title: Freakonomics
author: Steven D. Levitt & Stephen J. Dubner
synopsis: An economist applies his tools to everyday questions, uncovering surprising hidden patterns behind human behavior.

title: The Tipping Point
author: Malcolm Gladwell
synopsis: An exploration of how small changes can trigger widespread trends, ideas, and social behaviors.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
synopsis: A practical guide to building good habits and breaking bad ones through small, consistent changes.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
synopsis: A Nobel laureate examines the two systems that drive how people think, decide, and make judgments.

title: Guns, Germs, and Steel
author: Jared Diamond
synopsis: An examination of how geography, agriculture, and disease shaped the divergent fates of human societies.

title: A People's History of the United States
author: Howard Zinn
synopsis: American history retold from the perspective of workers, women, and minorities often left out of traditional accounts.

author: David McCullough
synopsis: A detailed account of the pivotal year of the American Revolution, from the siege of Boston to the crossing of the Delaware.
